In baseball terminology, "ball four" refers to when a batter receives four pitches that the umpire calls as balls, which results in a walk (also known as a base on balls). The batter then proceeds to first base without the threat of being put out. So, "took ball four" would mean that Juan Soto walked on four balls during a plate appearance. This is a common occurrence in baseball and is a standard way to describe such an event. Let me check if there's any reason this wouldn't be plausible. In baseball jargon, "taking a walk" is a common expression for receiving four balls. So "took ball four" is a concise and accurate way to describe this.
